items.title: Phase 1/2 study of EGFR-targeted combination therapy after osimertinib resistance in metastatic NSCLC
items.summary: Recruiting multi-site study for metastatic non-small cell lung cancer with acquired resistance after osimertinib. Sites include Dallas and Houston. Biomarker enrichment includes EGFR-mutated disease and resistance pathway evaluation.
gaps: Eligibility depends on detailed resistance testing, performance status, and prior line-of-therapy review.
items.evidence_snippet: Recruiting study for metastatic NSCLC after osimertinib progression with biomarker-driven eligibility review.

items.title: Basket trial for MET amplification after targeted therapy progression
items.summary: Early-phase basket study for solid tumors with MET amplification or MET-driven resistance. Enrollment may require fresh tissue or circulating tumor DNA confirmation.
gaps: Requires specific resistance confirmation and travel feasibility review.
items.evidence_snippet: Fresh tissue or ctDNA confirmation may be required before formal screening.

items.title: Class safety reminder: monitor for interstitial lung disease symptoms during EGFR inhibitor therapy
items.summary: Demo safety update reminding clinicians and patients to discuss cough, shortness of breath, or unexplained fever promptly during EGFR-targeted therapy. This is a monitoring-oriented note, not a treatment instruction.
gaps: Clinical interpretation depends on the exact drug, symptoms, and oncology context.
items.evidence_snippet: Respiratory symptoms during EGFR-targeted therapy should be reviewed urgently with the care team.

items.title: Review summary: C797S and MET amplification as common acquired resistance themes after osimertinib
items.summary: Demo biomarker review highlighting acquired EGFR C797S and MET amplification as resistance themes that may influence what is worth discussing with the oncology team after progression.
gaps: Needs real molecular testing context and clinician interpretation.
items.evidence_snippet: Acquired resistance patterns often require updated tissue or liquid biopsy context.
